subject: [RFC PATCH 4/5] mm, swap: only charge physical swap entries

2026-05-28.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's memory management to only charge physical swap entries against memcg->swap, rather than double-charging them at vswap allocation and when physical backing is allocated. The patch splits the lifecycle of a swap entry into four operations: recording the memcg private ID without charging, charging memcg->swap when physical backing is allocated, uncharging in vswap_release_backing, and dropping the ID ref at __swap_cluster_free_entries. This change aims to fix an issue where vswap entries were being double-charged against memcg->swap.
